qas.q: What is the difference between csu/dsu and a modem
qas.a: Csu/dsu converts a digital signal from a router to a leased line; a modem converts digital signals from a router to a phone line

qas.q: Describe the router bootup process
qas.a:
Post checks the routers hardware

Configuration register will identify where to load the ios image


After the ios image is stored the router loads the configuration file into ram

qas.q: Where does routing occur within the dod tcp/ip reference model
Before installing a new ios, should you check the rom or ram
qas.a: Ram, through the show version command

qas.q: Which layer in the osi reference model is responsible for determining the availability if the receiving program and checking to see if enough resources exist for that communication
How does service password encryption command on a router encrypt
qas.a: By encrypting passwords in plain text configuration file

qas.q: What is a global command
qas.a: A command that can affect the entire router

qas.q: What command would you use to allow only one telnet session an encrypt the password that would appear on a show command
qas.a:
Service password-encryption

Line vty 1

Login

Password

qas.q: Which PPP sub protocol negotiations authentication options
Which layer 2 protocol supports synchronous and asynchronous circuits and has built in security mechanisms
What are some examples of synchronous and asynchronous circuits
qas.a:
Synchronous would be analog (like analog phone lines)

Asynchronous circuits like isdn or digital links

qas.q: Describe characteristics of a csu/dsu and a modem
qas.a:
Csu/dsu terminates a digital local loop

A modem terminates an analog loops

qas.q: Is a router commonly considered a dte or dce device
When does chap authentication normally occur
When a switch receives a frame with an unknown MAC destination, does it arp or flood
How do layer 2 Ethernet switches decrease the number of collisions on a network
Why will a switch never learn from a broadcast address
qas.a: Because it will never be the source address of a frame

qas.q: What value is used to determine which port becomes the root port on each non root switch
What three things are true about rstp
qas.a:
Rstp reduces topology the reconvergance after a link failure

Rstp expands the stp port roles by adding the alternate and backup roles

Rstp provides a faster transition to the forwarding state on point to point links than stp does

qas.q: Which command enables rstp on a switch
What acl would you use if you want to allow a temporary entry for a remote user with a specific username and password so that the user can access the entire network over the internet
How long does a static nat translation stay in the nat table
T or f: static nat. Translations allows connections to be initiated from the outside
What is the vlan range that can be removed from a switch
How does a dhcp server handle ip dhcp conflicts
qas.a: The address is removed from the pool until the conflict is resolved

qas.q: If a dhcp conflict occurs, what must be done to resolve it
qas.a: An admin must resolve the conflict

qas.q: How does ospf allow for extensive control of routing
qas.a: Admins can filter lsa's to meet their requirements

qas.q: Why would an ospf entry be missing from a routing table
What command can you use to change the equal cost paths
What is the maximum number of equal cost paths that routers can have
True or false: with ospf, your hello and dead intervals should be the same
Which command is used to display the collection of ospf link states
What protocol is an open standard protocol framework that is commonly used in VPN to provide secure end to end connections
What are the three reasons that an organization might use cisco VPN solution instead of a point to point wan links
For PPP authentication, what must match between 2 different but directly connected router
Not setting a clock command will produce what type of code on the show ip interface brief command
What 3 things would cause a layer 2 problem between 2 connected routers
qas.a:
Keepalive mismatch

Encapsulation mismatch

Clocking problem

qas.q: What are three characteristics of an ipv6 Anycast address
qas.a:
One to nearest communication model

The same address for multiple devices in the group

Delivery of packets to the group interface that is closest to the sending device

qas.q: What 3 approaches can u use to migrate ipv4 to ipv6
qas.a:
Configuring ipv4 tunnels between ipv6 islands

Use proxying and translation (nat-pt) to translate ipv6 packets into ipv4 packets

Enable dual stacking

qas.q: Which two ipv6 addresses can be used for unicast routing
What is known as one to nearest addressing in ipv6
What does chap help with
qas.a: Verify the identity of the peer by means of a three way handshake

qas.q: What will happen if you have two matching static routes
qas.a: The more specific route (the one with the most 1's) will be chose to route a packet

What are the advantages and disadVantages of a circuit switch
